author: y4rr
content:
Result: added the foundational layer for meta-device tensors in ferrotorch-core. Mirrors `torch.device('meta')`.

**Device enum** (device.rs): added `Device::Meta` variant + `is_meta()` helper. Display impl prints `meta`.

**Storage** (storage.rs):
- New `StorageBuffer::Meta { numel, _phantom: PhantomData<T> }` variant — records element count only, no backing buffer
- New `TensorStorage::meta(numel)` constructor
- New `TensorStorage::is_meta()` method
- `on_device` and `on_device_pinned` now accept `Device::Meta` as a target — they discard the input data and return a meta storage with the right size
- All 24+ existing match arms in storage.rs handle the new variant explicitly: len() returns numel; as_slice/as_mut_slice panic with a meta-tensor message; gpu_handle returns None; try_clone and try_clone_subregion build a fresh meta storage of the right size; Clone impl forwards to meta(); Debug prints `Meta(N elements)`
- Drop is unchanged — meta storage has no resources to release

**Tensor API** (tensor.rs):
- New `Tensor::is_meta()` method
- `Tensor::data()` and `Tensor::data_vec()` now return a clear InvalidArgument error for meta tensors with the message: 'cannot read data from a meta tensor; meta tensors carry shape only. Call .to(Device::Cpu) to materialize, or use .shape() / .numel() / .device() for metadata access.'
- `Tensor::to(Device::Meta)` from any source device drops the data and returns a meta tensor with the same shape
- `Tensor::to(other)` from a meta tensor returns an InvalidArgument error pointing the user at creation::zeros/randn/etc. on the target device

**Creation funcs** (creation.rs):
- `zeros_meta(shape)`, `ones_meta(shape)`, `full_meta(shape, value)` — all build a meta tensor with the given shape (the value is ignored since meta carries no data)
- `meta_like(other)` — meta tensor matching the shape of an existing tensor

**Other crates touched:** ferrotorch-core/src/autograd/checkpoint.rs had a non-exhaustive match on Device that needed a Meta arm (returns None — no GPU RNG state to save for meta).

**Tests added (10 in creation.rs):**
- Basic shape and is_meta()
- Huge shape construction (10000x10000 = 100M elements) without allocation, proves meta tensors really don't alloc
- data() and data_vec() return clear meta-tensor errors
- meta_like matches shape and reports is_meta correctly
- to(Device::Meta) from CPU drops data without affecting the original
- to(Device::Cpu) from meta errors with the expected message
- Display shows 'meta'
- Cloning a 1G-element meta tensor doesn't allocate
- ones_meta and full_meta alias zeros_meta (since data is irrelevant)

888 ferrotorch-core lib tests pass (was 878, +10). `cargo check --workspace` is clean with no new warnings.

**Scope note:** This issue lands the FOUNDATION (storage + creation + metadata access). Operations on meta tensors (arithmetic, linalg, conv, etc.) currently error rather than propagating shapes — for full dry-run model construction, ops also need to recognize meta inputs and compute output shapes without touching data. Filed as follow-up #500 with the per-op-category checklist.
